segments.text:
Put the trumpet to your lips! Something like an eagle is over Yahweh’s house, 
because they have broken my covenant
and rebelled against my law.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: They cry to me, ‘My God, we, Israel, acknowledge you!’
segments.speaker: Israel

segments.text:
Israel has cast off that which is good. 
The enemy will pursue him.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
They have set up kings, but not by me. They have made princes, and I didn’t approve. 
Of their silver and their gold they have made themselves idols, 
that they may be cut off.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text:
Let Samaria throw out his calf idol! My anger burns against them! 
How long will it be until they are capable of purity?
segments.speaker: God

segments.text:
For this is even from Israel! The workman made it, and it is no God; 
indeed, the calf of Samaria shall be broken in pieces.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
For they sow the wind, and they will reap the whirlwind. He has no standing grain. The stalk will yield no head. 
If it does yield, strangers will swallow it up.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Israel is swallowed up. Now they are among the nations like a worthless thing.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
For they have gone up to Assyria, like a wild donkey wandering alone. 
Ephraim has hired lovers for himself.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text:
But although they sold themselves among the nations, I will now gather them; 
and they begin to waste away because of the oppression of the king of mighty ones.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text: Because Ephraim has multiplied altars for sinning, they became for him altars for sinning.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: I wrote for him the many things of my law, but they were regarded as a strange thing.
segments.speaker: God

segments.text:
As for the sacrifices of my offerings, they sacrifice meat and eat it, 
but Yahweh doesn’t accept them. Now he will remember their iniquity, and punish their sins. 
They will return to Egypt.
segments.speaker: God
God
